hero.sub: All 128 official questions, one at a time — no multiple choice, no clutter. Say your answer out loud, then check yourself. Free to start, no subscription ever.

problem.conclusion: This app is built for the second column.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps. Just like the real interview.
howItWorks.step1Body: One civics question at a time, big and clear — exactly the way a USCIS officer reads it.
howItWorks.step2Body: No options to tap. Speak your answer — or tap the speaker to hear the question first. The real test is oral, so practice that way.
howItWorks.step3Body: Tap to reveal the answer. Got it? Mark it confident. Still shaky? Mark it needs practice. Your progress is saved automatically.
howItWorks.step4Body: 20 random questions, oral-style, with a pass/fail result at the end — the same format as your naturalization interview.

features.h2: Built around the real exam format.

oral.title: Oral format, not multiple choice
oral.body: See the question, say your answer out loud, then check yourself. No options to tap. This is how the real interview works.

audio.title: Hear the question spoken
audio.body: Tap the speaker on any question to hear it read aloud. Train your ear for the exact phrasing a USCIS officer uses.

track.title: Track what you know
track.body: Mark each answer confident or needs practice. Your progress is saved and synced so you always know where you stand.

mock.title: Mock interview
mock.body: 20 random questions, oral-style, with a pass/fail result at the end — the same format as your naturalization interview.

pricing.h2: Free to study. One-time to unlock.
pricing.footnote: Most civics apps charge $10+ / month. You pay $12.99 once here — then you’re done.

rule6520.h2: 65+ and a permanent resident for 20+ years?
rule6520.body: You only have to study the 20 starred questions. The officer asks 10, and you need 6 right to pass. The app has a dedicated 65/20 study mode.

founder.name: Michael Downey
founder.quote: In April 2026 I was studying for my own citizenship interview. Every app I downloaded was multiple choice — that’s not how the test works. I found a YouTube channel where someone would ask the question, pause, then give the answer. That was the closest thing to real oral practice I could find. So I built something better. I passed 12 out of 12 on May 20.

faq.q1: How many questions are on the USCIS civics test?
faq.a1: There are 128 official USCIS civics questions. During your naturalization interview, the officer will ask up to 20 of them.
faq.q2: How many do I need to get right to pass?
faq.a2: You need to answer 12 out of 20 questions correctly to pass.
faq.q3: Is the civics test oral or written?
faq.a3: It is oral. A USCIS officer asks you questions and you answer out loud. There are no multiple choice options.
faq.a4: If you are 65 or older and have been a permanent resident for 20+ years, you only need to study 20 specially marked questions. The officer asks 10, and you need 6 correct to pass. The app has a dedicated mode for this.
faq.q5: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a5: No. You can try the app without an account. Creating a free account lets you save your progress and track which questions you have mastered.

cta.sub: Start free. Unlock unlimited mock tests for $12.99 — one time, no subscription.
